title: Some Useful Signal Models
summary: This page explains the complex frequency plane (s-plane), where the real part σ (neper frequency) indicates the rate of amplitude change and the imaginary part ω (radian frequency) indicates oscillation frequency. Signals in the left half-plane (LHP) correspond to exponentially decaying signals, those in the right half-plane (RHP) to exponentially growing signals, and those on the imaginary axis to constant-amplitude sinusoids. Examples illustrate how exponentially growing and decaying sinusoids correspond to complex frequencies in the RHP and LHP respectively.

figures.caption: The complex frequency plane (s-plane) showing the imaginary axis (jω), real axis (σ), left half-plane associated with exponentially decreasing signals, and right half-plane associated with exponentially increasing signals.
figures.description: The figure shows the complex frequency plane (s-plane) with a horizontal real axis labeled σ and a vertical imaginary axis labeled jω. The plane is divided into a left half-plane and a right half-plane, with text indicating that points in the left half-plane correspond to exponentially decreasing signals and points in the right half-plane correspond to exponentially increasing signals. Students should notice that the sign of the real part σ determines decay versus growth, while the imaginary axis represents oscillatory frequency.
